title: Hardcoded Owner Address Creates Single Point of Failure
reason: This is a centralization concern, not a standalone exploitable bug, and in this codebase `maxSupply` is never enforced anyway, so controlling `setMaxSupply()` does not directly affect token behavior.

title: Token Name and Symbol Mismatch with ERC20 Standard
reason: This is a metadata inconsistency, not a protocol-level security issue causing fund loss, insolvency, lockup, or realistic DoS.

title: Registry getContractAddress Reverts Instead of Returning Safe Default
reason: Failing closed on unknown keys is expected behavior. Any mint DoS from a bad registry is already subsumed by the unrestricted `setRegistry()` issue; without that, this mainly reflects owner misconfiguration rather than an independent exploit.
